pregame_model_read.pre_bet_thesis: deGrom 7+ at +118 was the cleanest board edge. The Cardinals matchup was not a blind smash spot due to possible pitch-count drag, but deGrom's chase/whiff profile and median-aligned 7+ threshold preserved strong value. The model avoided 8+/9+ ladder exposure.

pitch_notes.four_seam_fastball: 40 pitches, 1 K, 97.6 mph avg, 25% CSW. Fastball carried velocity but was not the main strikeout finisher.
pitch_notes.slider: 24 pitches, 3 K, 40% whiffs per swing, 29% CSW. Primary putaway weapon vs right-handed bats.
pitch_notes.changeup: 17 pitches, 2 K, 67% whiffs per swing, 35% CSW. Strong secondary whiff driver, especially vs left-handed bats.
pitch_notes.curveball: 10 pitches, 2 K, 70% CSW. Small-volume but elite finish/steal-strike weapon.

what_worked: The model correctly held the 7+ threshold and avoided overextending to 8+/9+ despite plus pricing.
deGrom finished with 8 strikeouts in only 5.0 innings, confirming K efficiency even with pitch-count drag.
Whiff quality was excellent: 18 whiffs and 38% whiff rate on swings.
CSW reached 33%, clearing the green-zone threshold.
The slider/changeup/curveball group generated 7 of the 8 strikeouts, validating the pitch-mix thesis.
risks_that_showed: Pitch count reached 91 after only 5.0 innings, confirming pregame concerns about Cardinals pitch-count drag.
Hard contact was present: 8 hard-hit balls out of 12 BBE and 96.0 average EV allowed.
Fastball was velocity-positive but not a dominant K finisher in this outing.
model_evaluation.patch_recommendation: KEEP deGrom 7+ as actionable when market prices him at plus money near median. Maintain anti-burn restriction against 8+/9+ ladder adds unless live pitch count is efficient and CSW is nuclear through 2 innings.

notes: Clean threshold win. The Cardinals matchup created pitch-count pressure exactly as flagged, but deGrom's secondary pitch whiff profile overcame it. The result validates the decision to bet 7+ only and avoid ladder exposure.
